channel.title: math.GN updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: math.GN updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: On the center of distances of finite ultrametric spaces
item.description: arXiv:2603.25850v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: The center of distances of a metric space $(X,d)$ is the set $C(X)$ of all $t\in \mathbb R^+$ for which the equation $d(x,p)=t$ has a solution for each $p\in X$. We prove the inequality $|C(X)| \le 1 + \lfloor \log_2 n \rfloor$ for all finite ultrametric spaces $(X,d)$ which have exactly $n$ points. It is also shown that for every integer $n \geq 1$ there exists a finite ultrametric space $(Y,\rho)$ such that $|Y| = n$ and $|C(Y)| = 1 + \log_2 \lfloor n \rfloor. $
item.dc:creator: Oleksiy Dovgoshey, Olga Rovenska

item.title: On exact and totally bounded capacities
item.description: arXiv:2603.20478v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We consider capacity (fuzzy measure, non-additive probability) on a compactum as a monotone cooperative normed game. We introduce topological analogues of well known classes of exact and totally balanced games and show that these classes form subfunctors of the capacity functor which lie between known subfunctors of convex capacities and balanced capacities. It is natural to consider probability measures as elements of core of such games. We describes exact capacities as a retraction of the convex closed sets of probability measures. Using such representation we prove openness of the functor of exact capacities.
item.dc:creator: Taras Radul
